pattern: Every day-ordinal suffix in this calendar is exactly two letters long, so stripping the last two characters of the day token always isolates its digits regardless of whether the day itself is one or two digits.
hints: Split the string on spaces to get three tokens: the ordinal day, the three-letter month abbreviation, and the four-digit year.

Every ordinal suffix (st, nd, rd, th) is exactly two characters, so the digits of the day are whatever remains after dropping the last two characters of that token, regardless of whether the day is one or two digits.
Map the three-letter month abbreviation to its 1-12 position with a fixed lookup table, then zero-pad the month and day to two digits and join as YYYY-MM-DD.

edge_inputs.description: two-digit day in the middle of the year
single-digit day that must be zero-padded on output
latest allowed year, two-digit day, December
single-digit day with an 'nd' suffix, not just 'st'/'th'

stress.description: random valid dates across the full year range and every month
